block_overview: The AXI_DMA is a single-channel Direct Memory Access controller designed for memory-to-memory data transfers. It exposes an AXI4 master interface for performing source and destination memory accesses, and an APB slave interface for software configuration and status readback. The block supports configurable transfer sizes (1–65535 bytes), INCR-type burst transfers of up to 16 beats, and generates interrupts on transfer completion or AXI error. Scatter-gather is explicitly not supported in this version (v0.9 Draft). The block is positioned as a simple, single-outstanding-transaction DMA engine suitable for use in SoC subsystems requiring lightweight memory-copy acceleration.

features.name: AXI4 Master Interface
features.description: AXI4 master port used for both source (read) and destination (write) memory access

subfeatures.name: Write Channel
subfeatures.description: Supports AW, W, and B AXI4 channels for memory write operations

subfeatures.name: Read Channel
subfeatures.description: Supports AR and R AXI4 channels for memory read operations

features.name: APB Slave Interface
features.description: APB slave port for register-based configuration and status

features.name: Configurable Transfer Size
features.description: Transfer length is programmable from 1 to 65535 bytes

subfeatures.name: Byte Granularity
subfeatures.description: Transfer size specified in bytes, range 1–65535

features.name: Burst Transfers
features.description: Supports INCR burst type with configurable burst length of 1–16 beats

subfeatures.name: INCR Burst Type
subfeatures.description: Only INCR (incrementing address) burst type is supported

subfeatures.name: Max Burst Length
subfeatures.description: Maximum burst length configurable via MAX_BURST parameter, default 16 beats

features.name: Transfer Complete Interrupt
features.description: Generates an interrupt (dma_done) when a DMA transfer finishes successfully

features.name: Error Interrupt
features.description: Generates an interrupt (dma_err) when an AXI slave error response is received

subfeatures.name: Software Clear Required
subfeatures.description: Error state must be cleared by software before new transfers can proceed

features.name: No Scatter-Gather
features.description: Scatter-gather mode is not supported in this version

parameters.name: DATA_WIDTH
parameters.description: AXI data bus width in bits

parameters.name: ADDR_WIDTH
parameters.description: AXI address bus width in bits

parameters.name: MAX_BURST
parameters.description: Maximum AXI burst length in beats

clock_domains.name: aclk
clock_domains.affected_logic: AXI4 master interface, DMA datapath, transfer engine

clock_domains.name: pclk
clock_domains.affected_logic: APB slave interface, register file, configuration logic

operating_modes.name: Idle
operating_modes.description: No transfer in progress; DMA awaiting a new transfer request
operating_modes.configuration: Default state after reset; entered after transfer completion or error clear

operating_modes.name: Active Transfer
operating_modes.description: DMA is actively moving data from source address to destination address via AXI4
operating_modes.configuration: Initiated by software programming source/destination addresses and transfer length, then asserting start

operating_modes.name: Error State
operating_modes.description: An AXI slave error response was received; DMA halted
operating_modes.configuration: Entered automatically on AXI error; exit requires software clear via register write

compliance_standards.version: AMBA AXI and ACE Protocol Specification ARM IHI0022
compliance_standards.notes: Master interface only; INCR burst type; single outstanding transaction

known_constraints: Only memory-to-memory transfers are supported; peripheral-to-memory and memory-to-peripheral are not supported
Only a single outstanding AXI transaction is permitted at a time; no transaction pipelining
Source and destination addresses must be word-aligned (32-bit / 4-byte aligned)
Scatter-gather mode is not supported in this version
Maximum burst length is 16 beats (INCR type only)
Error state requires explicit software clear before a new transfer can be initiated
needs_review: Register Map: The spec does not define the APB register map (addresses, register names, bitfields for start, source address, destination address, transfer length, status, interrupt enable/clear). This is critical for DV and must be defined.
AXI ID signals (awid, arid, bid, rid): Not listed in the signal table. It is unclear whether the DUT uses a fixed ID or if these signals are present.
AXI Size signals (awsize, arsize): Not listed. It is unclear whether these are fixed to the full data width or configurable.
AXI Burst type signals (awburst, arburst): Not listed explicitly, though the spec states INCR type is used. Presence in the port list should be confirmed.
AXI Protection/Cache/Lock signals (awprot, arprot, awcache, arcache, awlock, arlock): Not listed. Their presence or absence should be confirmed.

Clock domain crossing (CDC): The spec defines two separate clock domains (aclk and pclk) but does not describe any CDC mechanism between them. The synchronization strategy for register writes from pclk domain to aclk domain needs clarification.
Interrupt clear mechanism: dma_done and dma_err interrupt outputs are described but the clear mechanism (pulse vs. level, write-1-to-clear, etc.) is not specified.
Operating mode start mechanism: The Active Transfer mode mentions software programming registers, but the exact trigger mechanism (e.g., a START bit in a control register) is not specified.
AXI WSTRB behavior: The wstrb signal is listed but its behavior for sub-word or unaligned transfers is not described.
